title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: AI investments surge in India as tech leaders convene for Delhi summit
items.description: Blackstone, AMD, and Tata Consultancy Services unveil major infrastructure plans as OpenAI and Anthropic focus on India growth.

items.title: A Holiday High-School Game Is Shattered by a Fatal Assault
items.description: At a hockey rink in Pawtucket, R.I., shots rang out, leaving two people dead, in addition to the shooter. The police chief said it was a family matter...

items.title: New Sign at Bridge to Canada Aims to Keep Immigrant Drivers Out of Trap
items.description: The recent detention of a visiting research scientist has renewed attention on a confusing exit ramp in Buffalo that sends drivers across the border.

items.title: For success in AI, avoid the ‘efficiency trap’— and focus on trust instead
items.description: Trust can make or break success in AI implementation.

items.title: Jesse Jackson, Charismatic Champion of Civil Rights, Dies at 84
items.description: An impassioned orator, he was a moral and political force, forming a “rainbow coalition” of poor and working-class people and seeking the presidency. ...

items.title: As boomer and Gen X bosses retire, working from home will make a major comeback, new research predicts
items.description: A new study of 8,000 workers confirms that Gen Z CEOs are set to bring back working from home as they take over—turning today’s office mandates into a...

items.title: D-Wave CEO shrugs off short attacks with ‘revolutionary’ $550 million quantum computing acquisition
items.description: Less than 10% of D-Wave's clients are government research contracts, Alan Baratz says, proof it is offering commercially viable services.

items.title: U.S. and Iran Make ‘Good Progress’ in Geneva Talks, Foreign Minister Says
items.description: President Trump has called on Iran to reach an immediate accord or else face the threat of a possible U.S. attack.

items.title: Iranians Defy Government Crackdown at Memorials for Slain Protesters
items.description: Authorities moved to suppress shows of defiance at ceremonies commemorating 40 days since the death of protesters.

items.title: While big tech burns cash on AI, Apple waits
items.description: The company's apparent absence from the AI race may be its most disciplined strategic decision in years.

items.title: Ukraine and Russia Hold New Round of Peace Talks, but Expectations Are Low
items.description: Both sides described previous U.S.-mediated negotiations as productive, although they did not appear to address sticking points like territory and sec...

items.title: U.S. and Iran Make ‘Good Progress’ in Geneva Talks, Foreign Minister Says
items.description: President Trump has called on Iran to reach an immediate accord or else face the threat of a possible U.S. attack.

items.title: U.S. and Iran’s High-Stakes Nuclear Talks Set to Kick Off
items.description: The U.S. and Iran have been flexing their military might as negotiators from the two countries resume nuclear talks in Switzerland.

items.title: In Iran, Slain Protesters’ Memorials Will Test State Crackdown
items.description: Ceremonies commemorating the 40 days after protesters were killed are planned this week, challenging the authorities’ ability to restrain them.

items.title: Japan Wins Pairs Figure Skating, Knocking China From the Top
items.description: Riku Miura and Ryuichi Kihara of Japan emerged with the gold medal with a flawless, high-scoring performance after Monday’s free skate.
